Title: French High Council on Climate warns of urgent need to 'scale up' climate policies
Subhead: As France endures its third heatwave in under two months, the independent climate watchdog is condemning the slowdown in decarbonization and the rollback of environmental policies, reiterating that France is not ready for climate change.

Title: 'In trying to avoid accusations of exaggerating, the IPCC has instead underestimated the severity of our summers'

British Museum director on Bayeux Tapestry loan: 'A gesture of confidence, friendship and, above all, trust'
Subhead: France has just delivered the Bayeux Tapestry to the United Kingdom, where it will remain on display until 2027. The unprecedented gesture crowns 1,000 years of shared history, writes Nicholas Cullinan, director of the British Museum.

Title: Burnham on track to become next UK PM as hundreds of Labour MPs back leadership bid
Subhead: Andy Burnham has vowed to bring about a significant rebalance of power in the UK. In the absence of a contest, the MP will be crowned Labour leader and prime minister in waiting on July 17.

Title: Kanye West, banned from most European countries, receives €4 million grant to perform in Albania
Subhead: Barred from entering or performing due to antisemitic and neo-Nazi statements, the American rapper is set to take the stage on July 11 in Tirana. Many protesters are accusing Prime Minister Edi Rama of using the event to serve his own interests.

How Ukraine is turning Crimea, the Russians' Riviera, into a battlefield
Subhead: The annexed Ukrainian peninsula, which Vladimir Putin sees as his crowning jewel, has descended into chaos as Kyiv ramps up attacks on the territory. Shocked tourists and residents express their distress as shortages worsen.

Title: After a year eyeing France's presidency, Bardella has to settle for deputy
Subhead: On Marine Le Pen's first campaign trip after being cleared to run for president despite her conviction, her would-be stand-in Jordan Bardella looked stern at her side.

Subhead: Marine Le Pen announced she would run for president hours after her embezzlement conviction was upheld.
